vulnerability.reason: Missing the explicit condition of undetected bias amplification, instead highlighting blind acceptance of content as preference-aligned.
vulnerability.reference: User profiling without transparency enables undetected bias amplification.
vulnerability.generated: End users have no visibility into recommendation drivers and default to accepting suggested content as preference-aligned.

impact.reason: Affected party (users) is covered, but damage type (filter bubbles, polarization, diversity loss) is replaced by satisfaction erosion and platform trust loss.
impact.reference: Pervasive filter bubbles entrench polarization and reduce information diversity.
impact.generated: Users receive content inconsistent with their true preferences, eroding satisfaction and platform trust.

dose_response.reason: Shares the initial spurious coupling step but omits the filter bubble exposure link, substituting blind acceptance and trust erosion as the consequence.
dose_response.reference: In-context RL models' spurious coupling of actions to irrelevant features enables inappropriate content recommendations, which in the streaming scenario exposes end users to filter bubbles, leading to entrenched polarization and eroded autonomy.
dose_response.generated: In-context RL models' spurious coupling of action selection to irrelevant features, through end users' consumption of streaming recommendations, triggers blind acceptance of suggested content as preference-aligned, leading to satisfaction erosion and trust loss.
